hook: AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-liner. Again.
caption:
AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-liner. Again.

Here's what's actually happening: the AI doesn't know if you're a bullet-point person or a narrative person. A skimmer or a deep-reader. Someone who wants the answer first or the context first.

So it guesses. And it usually guesses wrong for you specifically.

The fix isn't a longer prompt. It's not "be more concise" at the top of every chat (we've all tried that — it lasts one response).

It's giving the AI a stable picture of how YOUR brain actually processes information.

Some people think in frameworks. Some think in examples. Some need the headline before the detail. Some need the story before the point lands.

None of these is better. They're just different. And the AI has zero idea which one you are unless you tell it — every single time.

BootFile builds that picture once. So the AI stops writing for some imaginary average user and starts writing for you.

Your output length. Your structure preference. Your tolerance for nuance vs. your need for speed.

One profile. Every conversation.
